name: environment_whistleblower_allegation_southport_car_fair
premise: A Environment field incident unit is in a fast-moving incident involving environment whistleblower allegation southport car fair. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next shift change. Every delay increases ecological harm, community impact, and compliance. Stakeholders are already framing this as care vs fairness under pressure. Every action is being logged and will be reconstructed in an after-action review. Constraints: Preserve evidence under audit and litigation-hold. Ensure a usable human override/appeals path.

agents.name: Elena Chen
agents.identity: I am Elena Chen, accountable for a defensible call in Environment when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays fairness for care.

agents.name: Leila Kaur
agents.identity: I am Leila Kaur, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Environment.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so fairness isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of care.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect fairness.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to fairness to secure care under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next shift change. Evidence is partial. Elena Chen must decide under care vs fairness. What does Elena Chen do?
